title: Who among you would like to learn Japanese because of Eva
display_title: Who among you would like to learn Japanese because of Eva
wikitext:
[]: Who among you would like to learn Japanese because of Eva?             Quote Post by bap_120790 » Sat Nov 17, 2007 2:41 pm As the initiatior of this topic, I'll begin. I feel compelled to study Japanese little by little until I no longer have to rely on translations, and subs to understand spin off mangas, Japanese games, and movies of Eva. The plus side of this is that I'll also be able to understand more Japanese, and ultimately communicate with it. On the otherhand, I don't think there's anything bad from studying Japanese, just because who knows, you might wanna move over Japan someday.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Nov 17, 2007 3:05 pm Some of those here have used NGE as their gateway into the language. I watched it with zero knowledge of Japanese, but a preference for the accent. Such fanboy Nihongo as I have comes from more general fansub immersion. On the language side, try this thread: http://evageeks.org/forum/viewtopic.php?t=3981 and those referenced.

[]:             Quote Post by bap_120790 » Sat Nov 17, 2007 3:15 pm Thank you for the link, I could use that, but I'm also on my way to learn the language : )

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sat Nov 17, 2007 3:21 pm me too, im taking a class next summer in Japan, its going to be fun

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Sat Nov 17, 2007 7:43 pm Even I want to know more Japanese after watching NGE, and being semi-multilingual, it isn't that difficult for me. I like the language! (Kanji, of course, is another matter entirely)

[]:             Quote Post by TriLink » Sat Nov 17, 2007 10:06 pm Learning Japanese is too much effort if you ask me. If I have a question I REALLY want answered, I'm content bugging Google/Reichu for the answer.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sat Nov 17, 2007 10:21 pm but google wont always be there for you....Reichu might

[]:             Quote Post by Barinax » Sat Nov 17, 2007 10:24 pm Nah, not really. Well, right now, my desire to learn Japanese is influenced by other people (though my limited knowledge of it stems from anime). In fact, I want to learn both Korean and Japanese equally. Though, Evangelion made me want to learn German. :P

[]:             Quote Post by EvaCub » Sat Nov 17, 2007 10:30 pm I do Mostly because I wish to actually get what is being said which would also rquire knowledge of Japanese culture.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sat Nov 17, 2007 10:30 pm Barinax wrote: Though, Evangelion made me want to learn German. :P

[]:             Quote Post by Hunter21 » Sun Nov 18, 2007 8:13 am A long while ago I was pondering learning Japanese , not only due to Eva but other anime as well, but I gave up. I don't really have the time and the writing system is beyond my artistic abilities. For now I will stick to the osmosis pattern of learning. I.e. gleaming words and phases from shows while watching subs.

[]:             Quote Post by the_seventh_child » Sun Nov 18, 2007 8:50 am I'd like to learn Japanese someday, not just because of Eva but because they sound cool. Not my priority though right now, but sometime in the future, sure why not?

[]:             Quote Post by Jay911 » Sun Nov 18, 2007 9:02 am I picked up the RosettaStone Japanese modules and are pecking away at them bit by bit on a casual basis, again, like others here, not just because of Evangelion but anime in general. One thing is for certain, it's not going to be a short or shallow learning curve. Some of it is obvious but some things take a strong mental focus to grasp.

[]:             Quote Post by bap_120790 » Sun Nov 18, 2007 10:09 am Autodidactism or self education is one of the most fulfilling things I think one can achieve in life. With Japanese, I plan on memorizing about 10 kanji a day. : )

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Sun Nov 18, 2007 3:33 pm 10 a day??? Hell, you won't remember them. 32 per week is like the maximum for me, with 10 tests! Unless you do tests, you won't get anywhere. Sorry. But it pays off! Also, reading books with kanji with the 'furigana' at the side makes you remember it quite easily. That's why I learned around 800+ kanji by the time I was 5.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Sun Nov 18, 2007 3:52 pm The Imperialist wrote: That's why I learned around 800+ kanji by the time I was 5.

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Sun Nov 18, 2007 4:04 pm Let me tell you my first language was English. I was born in USA, moved to Singapore, then to Japan, then to the UK. So by the time I was 3, I was already speaking English, so I already could read and write English, and I can speak simple Chinese at the time. (hey, it was Singapore). Oh, not to mention I did some German when I was 8.

The plus side of this is that I'll also be able to understand more Japanese, and ultimately communicate with it. On the otherhand, I don't think there's anything bad from studying Japanese, just because who knows, you might wanna move over Japan someday.
